mnemonics.title: Remember Properties of Relations
mnemonics.content: RST: Reflexive = loops to self; Symmetric = mirrors; Transitive = chain reaction; Antisymmetric = one-way street if distinct; Equivalence = R+S+T; Partial Order = R+Antisym+T

acronyms.usage: Three fundamental properties of relations used to classify them into equivalence and partial orders
Relation obtained by swapping each ordered pair: (a,b) becomes (b,a)
R composed with S gives pairs (a,c) where ∃b with (a,b)∈R and (b,c)∈S

visualTips.visualization: Draw a circle with each element having a self-loop arrow pointing to itself. All diagonal elements present = reflexive.
If there's an arrow from A to B, there's also an arrow from B to A. Like a two-way street or mirror image.
If you can go from A→B→C, you can directly go A→C. Chain of connections collapses to direct link.
One-way arrows only. If A→B exists, B→A cannot exist (unless A=B). Like a one-way street.
Partition the set into equivalence classes. Each class is a cluster where all elements are related to each other.

analogies.analogy: Every person knows themselves - self-awareness. Like looking in a mirror and seeing yourself.
A friendship: if A is friend with B, then B is friend with A. Two-way relationship.
If A is taller than B, and B is taller than C, then A is taller than C. Inheritance through a chain.
Parent-child relationship: if A is parent of B, then B cannot be parent of A. One-direction hierarchy.
Students in same class form equivalence class - all treated equally, can be grouped together.
Family tree ancestry: if A is ancestor of B and B is ancestor of C, then A is ancestor of C. Ordering with possible incomparables.
